title: TLS, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Key Operations · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible encryption and key-lifecycle contract with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in encryption and key-lifecycle contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2945 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate TLS, server encryption, KMS policy, context, and rotation evidence; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.

Inspect the Transport At Rest Client trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
